gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 reviewed the Databricks engine-probe job ownership inversion and found no code-level behavioral regressions. The only finding was the intentionally pending PR-evidence sidecar, which is now marked complete. Review specifically checked document and legacy monkeypatch isolation, direct config construction, matrix target parsing, release-safe validation, legacy __all__/star-import compatibility, pickle/slotted behavior, and recursion/leak risks.

issues.resolution: Recorded the completed GPT-5.5 review outcome and reran the PR-evidence validation gate.
issues.summary: The new PR-evidence sidecar was still marked pending, causing the full repository PR-evidence gate to fail.

scope: document Databricks engine-probe job implementation ownership
legacy Databricks engine-probe job compatibility wrapper
legacy module execution and helper monkeypatch migration hooks
verification: poetry run pytest tests/test_databricks_engine_probe_job.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q
find src tests -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 python -m py_compile
PYTHONPATH=src poetry run python -m document_kv_cache.pr_evidence --validate-directory pr-evidence
repository secret scan over README, package metadata, source, tests, Databricks helpers, PR evidence, and GitHub metadata
GPT-5.5 review of the ownership migration with no code-level findings
what_changed: Moved the Databricks native engine-probe job implementation into document_kv_cache.databricks_engine_probe_job.
Turned restaurant_kv_serving.databricks_engine_probe_job into a compatibility wrapper over the document module.

Preserved legacy module execution, direct writer monkeypatch behavior, public and private helper monkeypatch behavior, pickle support, slotted config layout, and the previous legacy star-import surface.
Recorded document ownership in public package tests and the legacy package README.
why: The generalized package should own the Databricks job entry point that emits native vLLM/SGLang engine-probe evidence. Keeping legacy compatibility makes this an incremental, behavior-preserving step toward retiring restaurant-specific implementation ownership.
